    The API fabric enhances developer productivity by centralizing SAP and third-party APIs in a unified catalog. Developers can now quickly discover, register, and access a broad array of APIs, simplifying integration development and accelerating project timelines.

  • EOIO processing
    To support complex asynchronous integration scenarios, SAP Integration Suite now includes “exactly once in order” (EOIO) processing. This capability helps ensure messages are delivered exactly once and in the correct sequence while reducing data errors and enhancing consistency.

🔖Learn more about SAP Integration Suite's new features.

SAP Business AI: Making autonomous finance a reality

 The introduction of autonomous finance capabilities in SAP BTP is making a finance function that requires minimal human intervention a practical and productive reality. Enabled by the addition of SAP Business AI, this update automates complex financial operations—helping companies run with improved agility and resilience.

Leveraging the advanced AI and machine learning models of SAP Business AI, the platform provides SAP S/4HANA Cloud with tools that automate routine tasks, such as transaction processing and reconciliations. ​Recent benchmarking of SAP customers shows that adopting these capabilities paves the way to 90% more-efficient financial reporting, 7% higher forecast accuracy, and 10 times faster planning cycles.

In addition, the enhanced predictive capabilities and real-time data analytics available in SAP BTP equip SAP S/4HANA Cloud with insights that go beyond traditional finance automation. Organizations can now manage risk, increase transparency, and improve accuracy as the cloud ERP anticipates financial outcomes and adapts proactively to changes in cash flow or market volatility.

🔖Learn more about autonomous finance

SAP Build: Stepping up development with ABAP and generative AI

SAP Build solutions have become critical tools for the fast development of applications and automations with a simplified and streamlined builder interface.  Announced in October 2024, The latest integration of ABAP development tools and generative AI in SAP Build is making that experience even better for developers of all skill levels looking to modernize and extend their ERP capabilities.

By connecting tools such as ABAP, Java, JavaScript, and low-code in a unified environment, SAP Build enables direct development access within SAP S/4HANA Cloud, making customization faster and easier. The extensibility wizard allows extensions for fields, logic, and processes while maintaining core system integrity.

Prebuilt content in SAP Build accelerates work across functions, including HR, spend management, and procurement. For example, the Joule copilot boosts efficiency with code completion, workflow automation, and instant help access. Developers can also create custom AI skills with drag-and-drop simplicity.

🔖Learn more the latest in SAP Build for SAP S/4HANA

SAP Datasphere: Making data handling simpler and more reliable

SAP Datasphere focused on improving access to mission-critical business data across any cloud infrastructure with a business data fabric.  In July 2024, the following updates gave business users stronger control over data views, resources, and import flexibility.

  • Compatibility contracts
    By setting a data view’s release state to “Released,” business users can help ensure output columns remain consistent until they’re replaced by a successor. This feature also enables gradual decommissioning while maintaining system integrity.

  • Workload management
    Resource management can be centralized by defining priorities and statement limits for all spaces. In return, business users can manage workload distribution effectively, while deprecating parameters for the command-line interface simplify updates in future releases.

  • Space changes during content import
    Specific spaces can now be chosen during imports. Space mapping added flexibility to support intra-tenant transport. Business users can import content into different spaces within the same tenant, even creating multiple copies in various spaces if needed.

🔖Learn more about SAP Datasphere's new features

SAP Analytics Cloud: Improving insight depth and speed

SAP Analytics Cloud, also part of SAP BTP, has been enhanced with several powerful updates that streamline data analysis, planning, and presentation. With these new features, SAP Analytics Cloud is adding even more depth and flexibility for customers using SAP S/4HANA Cloud, making it easier for teams across the business to access insights and make data-driven decisions.

Here’s a look at some of the most impactful advancements:

  • Report-report interface
    Organizations with live connections to the SAP Business Warehouse (SAP BW) application can now leverage the report-report interface to analyze data in context. With this feature, users can jump from making queries in SAP BW—from generating ABAP reports and transactions to analyzing data.

  • Natural language queries
    Enabled by generative AI, the just ask feature of SAP Analytics Cloud allows users to ask questions in their natural language and receive instant data-driven answers in SAP S/4HANA Cloud while reducing their dependence on IT support. Recent updates include tools to simplify the user experience, such as the prompt guide, model control options for administrators, and advanced comparisons.

  • Plan-data files uploading
    Starting with the Q3 2024 release, business users are now able to enhance their planning efficiency by uploading their plan-data files from SAP S/4HANA Cloud directly into SAP Analytics Cloud. They can initiate uploads through triggers in stories, and administrators can set up data structures and upload jobs to help ensure data consistency.

  • Standard unit conversion
    The standard unit conversion feature simplifies data handling by allowing modelers and planners to use predefined conversion tables for measurements like length and weight. This capability helps ensure consistent unit representation across reports and stories, supporting data accuracy in SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

SAP Analytics Cloud (part 2): Extending planning and analysis business-wide

This year’s updates also brought powerful enhancements for planning and analysis in SAP Analytics Cloud. The following innovations were added to SAP Analytics Cloud to further streamline workflows, foster collaboration, and support strategic insights across departments, particulary for users with SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

  • Triggering of calendar events from external applications
    Enhancing workflow automation for enterprise planning scenarios, this capability simplifies end-to-end planning processes and helps ensure key tasks are executed without disruption across integrated platforms.

  • Automated synchronization of calendar events
    With automatic synchronization of SAP calendar events with Microsoft Teams and Outlook, users can keep track of critical planning deadlines and events. This integration improves productivity and collaboration, embedding SAP planning directly into users’ daily calendars.

  • Industry-specific business content
    New and updated content packages in SAP Analytics Cloud provide prebuilt templates and analytics tools for business areas, such as sustainability, finance, sales, and the public sector. These packages accelerate the implementation of SAP S/4HANA Cloud, allowing companies to leverage industry-specific insights more quickly and effectively.

  • Probability simulation
    The compass functionality of SAP Analytics Cloud facilitates probability simulations that allow organizations to anticipate risks and model scenarios across key business drivers. Decision-makers can simulate the best, worst, and realistic outcomes—leading to more-proactive strategies to mitigate emerging disruptions.

  • Continuous planning integration
    The integration feature for SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere forms a unified, governed data environment for planning and analytics. By centralizing data on one platform, this feature fosters collaboration across departments and reduces time-to-action for mission-critical decisions.

🔖Learn more about the new features in SAP Analytics Cloud

SAP HANA Cloud: Extending AI support and larger data volumes

 

SAP HANA Cloud is SAP’s database as a service solution, designed for large scale deployments.  The new features in SAP HANA Cloud for 2024 provide improved support for artificial intelligence and larger deployements, along with many quality of life features. Here are just a few of the highlights:

  • General availability of the Vector Engine for SAP HANA Cloud
    The introduction of the vector engine allows SAP HANA Cloud to provide the foundation for large language models, facilitating more contextually accurate responses to business queries. Learn more in this related blog post.

  • Support for larger instances
    With the 2024 Q2 release, the amount of memory available to allocate to a database instance increased to 12 TB. This allows better support for memory-intensive workloads.
  • Cloud Reliability – Backup Compression
    New backup compression for data, log, and catalog backups for SAP HANA database instances in SAP HANA Cloud reduces the amount of backup storage consumed, while ensuring the integrity and security of the backups.

  • Online migration
    The newly introduced online migration capabilities allow critical business processes to remain operational within the source SAP HANA system during the migration process. This innovation minimizes migration downtime, automates migration tasks, and reduces the need for manual interventions.

  • New machine learning options
    Multiple new features to the Predictive Analysis Library (PAL) in SAP HANA Cloud facilitates faster modeling and application of AutoML, and automate analytics processes. The features allow a single neural network model to predict multiple related target columns. Other enhancements include the hierarchical density-based clustering of data with noise (HDBSCAN) and support for clustering of vector data using the DBSCAN and HDBSCAN functions.
    Learn more in the blog post provided by Christoph Morgen, as well as in this community post
  • Support for AWS PrivateLink services
    This new capability enables users to connect applications running within an Amazon Web Services (AWS) to SAP HANA Cloud using private IP endpoints, enhancing security. Learn more in this demo video.

Many more new features are described by @thomashammer in his blog post series.  

🔖 Learn more about SAP HANA Cloud

Closing

These new features in SAP BTP don't just make it easier to get the most from the data and processes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud—they also build a solid foundation for growth and adaptability. When you use these tools together, you’re creating an ERP experience that’s ready for whatever comes next.

Learn more about how SAP BTP enhances SAP S/4HANA Cloud at sap.com/btp-for-s4hana.

 

Explore more related content in this series about SAP BTP with S/4HANA:

Data Migration

  • SAP S/4HANA Data Migration and Master Data Management Best Practices with SAP BTP

Extensions

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Extensions with SAP Build Best Practices: An Expert Roundtable
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Extensions and Clean Core with SAP BTP - A Best Practices Conversation

Artificial Intelligence

  • Artificial Intelligence (AI) for SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP: A deep dive
  • How SAP Business AI and Large Language Models are shaping the future of SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP

entry.title: VCH AFL – Installation, Upgrade, Troubleshooting
entry.content:
General

The Application Function Library (VCH AFL) is fundamental to AVC (Advanced Variant Configuration). It’s written in C++ and is technically a plug-in running on the SAP HANA DB script server. The VCH AFL evaluates all the object dependencies in a configuration model with processing mode “Advanced.” This concerns all dependencies not attached to bill of material items (BOM items), also called “high-level dependencies.”

The VCH AVC lifecycle is managed by SAP in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, so the following information applies only to S/4HANA (on-premise) and S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. Here, customers have to manage installation and upgrades themselves.

 

Installation

The VCH AFL can be downloaded from the software center (https://me.sap.com/softwarecenter). Search for the VCH AFL in Software Downloads and add the S/4HANA version, e.g., “VCH AFL 2023”. After that, filter for the appropriate SAP HANA DB version (= SAP HANA revision), e.g., 79.01.

Please note that the patch level of the VCH AFL does not relate to the patch level of SAP HANA: VCH AFL with patch level 2 and SAP HANA patch level 0 work perfectly fine together. However, you should always take the highest patch level available. A general AFL note explaining the versioning can be found here: https://me.sap.com/notes/1898497/E. How to install the VCH AFL and which post-installation steps are necessary are described in the S/4HANA installation guide. You can find this guide on the main page of the help portal (https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE). Scroll down to the section “Implement,” subsection “Guides”:

The Installation Guide points to the SAP HANA Server Installation and Update Guide: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_PLATFORM/2c1988d620e04368aa4103bf26f17727/7eb0167eb35e4e2885415205b8383584.html. The following note describes the manual installation of the VCH AFL: https://me.sap.com/notes/2987015 (S/4 Release 1909OP and following).

 

Update

When using the AVC, please always keep SAP HANA DB and VCH AFL up to date, as explained here: https://me.sap.com/notes/3318944. Bug fixes and performance improvements are usually only available with the latest revisions. When you update the SAP HANA revision, first uninstall the old VCH AFL, then update SAP HANA, and then install the new VCH AFL.

When upgrading the SAP HANA DB, you can look for the latest VCH AFL patches in the software center. The SAP HANA DB installer does not always contain the latest VCH AFL.

 

Configuration

You can also manually configure some parameters of the VCH AFL concerning memory allocation or deletion of expired sessions. For details, refer to this note: https://me.sap.com/notes/2551305/E.

 

Dos and Don’ts and Troubleshooting

Run report VCH_HL_AFL_CHECK in transaction SA38 to check whether VCH AFL is installed correctly. If an error occurs, scroll down to see the error message. If the report results in errors but you are sure to have followed the installation guide, check whether one of the scenarios described below applies. If not, please create a ticket on component LO-VCH-ENG-AFL.

  • For SAP HANA revisions up to 59.14 or up to 79.01 (depending on the code line), the following issue exists: If you restart the index server of the DB while the script server is still running, the VCH AFL stops working. You can see how long the servers have been running in transaction DBACOCKPIT under Configuration Services. If the script server is older than the index server, restart it. See also https://me.sap.com/notes/3511162. 
  • In S/4HANA releases up to 2021, the temporary tables used for communicating between the VCH AFL and AS ABAP (ABAP application server) can accumulate, resulting in significant memory consumption. Please see https://me.sap.com/notes/3218076/E for how to fix this.
  • When running S/4 2022 FPS 0, please apply note https://me.sap.com/notes/0003247490 to get the VCH AFL to work (this is also mentioned in the release notes).

 

Further SAP Notes

  • “Upgrade VCH AFL of Advanced Variant Configuration for Faster Communication Between ABAP and VCH AFL”: https://me.sap.com/notes/3504987. This note is the prerequisite for https://me.sap.com/notes/3504072 (see below).
  • “Composite note: AVC Performance”: https://me.sap.com/notes/3280331. This composite note also contains the following note.
  • “Introduction of 'One Parameter Interface for AFL Communication'”: https://me.sap.com/notes/3504072

entry.title: End of Year Celebration: SAP Inside Track 2024 - Transforming with SAP S/4HANA and RISE
entry.content:
Why This Event is a Must-Attend

At the heart of this year’s event are two compelling sessions that will provide you with real-world insights and actionable takeaways. These presentations are designed not only to highlight the success stories of our customers but also to give you the tools and strategies you need to make your own transformation smoother and more successful.

Session 1:S/4HANA RISE at WEL Networks: A Partner-Led Brownfield Transformation

For the first time ever, we’ll have Chris Puddle from WEL Networks and Paul Brown, Solution Architect at Deloitte, take the stage to share the story of their successful Brownfield transformation from SAP ECC to S/4HANA with RISE with SAP. This session will cover the entire journey—from project initiation and timelines to the real challenges and key milestones that helped them succeed.

You’ll learn:

  • How WEL Networks partnered with Deloitte to navigate the complexities of the transformation process.
  • What it means to undergo a Brownfield transformation in the context of S/4HANA and why it's crucial for those still on ECC.
  • Key insights, tips, and “gotchas” that can save you time and resources as you prepare for your own move to S/4HANA.

This session is perfect for anyone involved in or planning to embark on an S/4HANA transformation, especially as we approach the 2027 deadline for ECC migration.

Session 2: Unlock the Full Potential of Your GROW Entitlements: Maximize SAP S/4HANA Cloud Benefits

If you’re already using SAP S/4HANA Cloud or are considering the shift, then you’ll want to hear from Grace Melhuish, Enterprise Customer Success Partner at SAP. Grace will walk you through how to maximize your GROW with SAP entitlements to fully unlock the power of S/4HANA Cloud.

This session will focus on:

  • Continuous Quality Checks and Cloud ALM, helping you ensure your system is running smoothly and optimally at every stage.
  • How to leverage your SAP investment to drive business transformation, not just on the technical side, but in delivering real business value.
  • Tips on ensuring long-term success in the cloud and how you can utilize the full suite of services available to you.

Whether you’re just starting out with S/4HANA Cloud or looking to enhance your existing setup, this session will provide valuable insights into how to get the most out of your cloud investment.

Event Details:

📅Date: 4th December 2024
⏰Time: 4:30 PM onwards
📍Location: SAP Building, Queen Street

This is a free event, and we encourage you to bring your colleagues, friends, and peers who are also looking to accelerate their SAP journey. It’s a fantastic opportunity to connect with the SAP community, learn from experts, and set yourself up for success in 2025.

Register Your Interest Don’t wait—spaces are filling up fast! ⏳

A Huge Thank You to Our Community Before we dive into the event itself, we want to take a moment to thank you—our SAP community—for your incredible support over the past year. The success of our SAP Inside Track events wouldn’t be possible without your enthusiasm, feedback, and passion for learning and growing together.

We’ve listened to your feedback from our previous events and have made some exciting changes to this year’s format to ensure even more value and diversity. By moving away from a sole focus on BTP (Business Technology Platform), we’ve expanded our content to reach a broader audience and to better serve the needs of everyone on their SAP journey.

Let’s Close 2024 with a Bang! This event is the perfect opportunity to gather fresh insights, share experiences, and finish the year with actionable takeaways that will set you up for a successful 2025. We’re excited to welcome WEL Networks, Deloitte, and SAP experts to share their experiences, and we hope you’ll join us for a memorable end-of-year celebration.

Spread the word—this is an event you don’t want to miss!

See you on December 4th!

#SAPInsideTrack #S4HANA #RISEwithSAP #GROWwithSAP #TransformationJourney #CustomerStory #SAPCommunity #TechInnovation #LearningTogether #CloudSuccess #DigitalTransformation #EventReminder #FinishStrong #EndOfYearCelebration @jpagcamaran @LuisBecker1 @GDR_ANZ @gracemelhuish @JJSingh @christoph_christophersen @Murali_Shanmugham

entry.title: On-stack Clean Core Extensibility: Plain and Simple
entry.content:
One of the things our team has been told is that "We have to decide for ourselves what is clean and what is not". This has proven to be very unhelpful and not factual.

The truth is: there is no mystery as to what constitutes clean extensibility. SAP is actually surprisingly clear on this topic.

Consider the following diagram, which you've probably seen before:

Here we are considering primarily the on-stack section.

What are the tiers?

The definition of Tier 1 is all your developments that use only released-for-cloud objects, which makes those objects clean and therefore upgrade-safe.

(Objects that are released for cloud are guaranteed by SAP to have a stable interface, hence anything that uses them is guaranteed not to break in an upgrade).

Tier 2 is just a way for you to mitigate the cases where SAP may not have provided a released object, so you create a (temporary) wrapper as a workaround.

So, for example, creating a table that only refers to released data elements, is tier 1.

Creating a table that uses data elements that are not released would be tier 2. (Which is a bad example, because you would in that case probably just create the data elements yourself).

Just creating a table or other object does not suggest Tier 1 or Tier 2; it's whether the object uses released objects or not that decides in which tier it is.

A better example is if you create a CDS view: There, it is more likely that you might have to rely on some view of SAP that is not released. In that case, the recommendation is to create a wrapper around the unreleased object, mark the wrapper as released for cloud, and use that in your CDS view. That way, your view remains  clean (Tier 1), but the wrapper is in Tier 2.

Then, if and when SAP releases something you can use to replace your tier 2 object, you retire your wrapper  and swap it out with the released object.

Using this approach, you reduce the risk that you are creating objects that might cause issues with upgrades by making it easier to keep track of the state of "cleanness" of your system.

Tier 3 covers everything that SAP says you must not use, like Dynpro, WebDynpro, unreleased BAdIs, etc. But this is anyway more applicable to people who are migrating developments from an old system, such as in a brownfield scenario. In a greenfield scenario, by contrast, you will most likely avoid creating such developments to begin with. In a brownfield scenario, the idea is to progressively retire all your Tier 3 developments and replace them with Tier 1.

In the case of side-by-side extensibility, where you operate outside of the S/4 system or "off-stack", you can only consume released APIs anyway, which means there is no risk of breaking something in an upgrade, so that is clean by default.

What a released-for-cloud object is

To be clear, this is what we are talking about:

This is what you see when you go to the API State properties of an object that is released for cloud development.

When you make use of the "ABAP for Cloud Development" language version (a.k.a. "ABAP Cloud"), the compiler checks whether the objects you use have been released for cloud and will prevent you from compiling otherwise.

This prevents you from accidentally incorporating non-released objects in your code.

You can tell if an object uses this version of ABAP in the properties of the object:

If you set this language version on a package, objects created in that package will by default also use it, but you can override this per object to use the "Standard ABAP" language version, which would be the case for a wrapper object.

How this ties in with AEM (SAP Application Extension Methodology)

In short, it doesn't, really.

This is another rabbit hole we've been led down, believing that AEM is somehow supposed to help us identify what is clean core. But that is not its purpose.

AEM is a methodology that helps formulate an approach to deciding what technologies to use to fulfil a requirement. But the assumption is that you already know what is clean and what is not, and that is what I try to convey in simple terms above.

entry.title: India Business: Keeping Backup of books of Account Leveraging SAP Document and Reporting Compliance
entry.content:
Enhanced Record-Keeping Regulations for Indian Businesses

As of August 5, 2022, Indian businesses must adhere to new record-keeping regulations introduced by the Ministry of Corporate Affairs (MCA). These regulations, outlined in the Companies (Accounts) Fourth Amendment Rules, 2022, mandate that companies ensure their electronic records are accessible within India at all times. Additionally, businesses are required to maintain a daily backup of their books of account data within the country.

Key Requirements

  • Accessibility: Electronic records must be accessible within India at all times.
  • Daily Backup: A daily backup of books of account data must be stored within India.

Leveraging SAP S/4HANA for Compliance

For businesses using SAP S/4HANA, compliance can be streamlined by utilizing the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC) statutory reporting solution. This tool helps users extract the necessary books of account data from their SAP S/4HANA systems, ensuring they meet the new regulatory requirements.

Guidance and Support

For detailed guidance on how to implement these changes, SAP has provided SAP Note 3443920 .

Important Note: Please be aware that the information provided here is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal or regulatory advice. Businesses should consult with their legal advisors to ensure full compliance with the new regulations.

If you have any questions or need further assistance, feel free to reach out. We're here to help you navigate these new requirements smoothly.

Karthiheyini Subbiah

entry.title: CIO Guide: Solutions to the growing hidden cost of Simplification in the Digital Transformation
entry.content:
In the past a Person might have used 3 or 4 Applications to do their Job, they might have taken Data from one application, copied it to Excel, manipulated it, and then put the Data  "in to SAP" for the final part of a Business Process, for example Accounts Receivable.

With the Digital Transformation, with the Intelligent Enterprise, and Simplification, the goal is that the 3 or 4 Applications are being replaced by 1 Application, 1 Front-End, 1 User-Experience, where People can do their Job altogether in the same place the same Application.

The hidden cost of this, which all SAP Technical Architects, SAP Integration Architects, SAP Security Architects, and SAP Data Architects will be very familiar with, is as we make the Front-End simpler, we make the Back-End exponentially more complicated.

This guide will introduce some options for simplifying the Back-End.

In the not too distant past, many people in your Company would use several Applications to do perform their Role, especially for example in Accounts, like this:

In the journey that we are all on the Simplify, as part of the Digital Transformation and the Intelligent Enterprise, one of the goals is going towards one "Application", one point of entry, where people can perform their Role from one place, a big part of that for many SAP Customers is S/4HANA, and the SAP BTP Work Zone.

This transformation, from many Applications to 1 Application can look something like this, as @Vera_Gutbrod shows in her excellent blog:

 

 

When the End User Experience is simplified for Business Processes which cross over multiple Applications and on top of that, Applications from different Vendors, the hidden price of this Front-End simplification is Back-End complexity.

What I am seeing, is that as the Front-End becomes more simple the Back-End complexity is going up exponentially:

 

Why is this happening, why is simplifying the Front-End, the End-User Experience making the Back-End so complicated ?

There are several dimensions driving the complexity, and maybe you can think of more, to make the Front-End simpler we need to:

. Integrate Applications

. Move Data

. Secure Connectivity & Access & Authorisation across multiple Applications and layers and the Data

. We need to do Mapping and Transformations of Data, either at the SAP End or the other End - this is another challenge to the "Clean-Core" and the S/4 Transformations, there needs to be a principle, mappings/transformation must happen outside of the S/4

. Compliance Requirements

Anybody involved in this kind of work will know how much of their time is being taken these days in Integration and Security Architecture discussions to enable Integrations.

From my experience, this is a lot of work, by a lot of people and a lot of effort, and this is the hidden price of End-User & Front-End Simplification, because, nobody sees all of this effort, apart from the people involved.

So how can we reduce these hidden costs ?

We need to look at the latest technologies and methods, here are some starting points:

. Event based Integrations with SAP Event Mesh and SAP Advanced Event Mesh - publish the data and let the subscribing applications react accordingly

. API Integrations with SAP Integration Suite, Cloud Integration, SAP APIM

. Simplified Security with Networking 2.0  - look at atsign dot com and noports dot com ,  look at how they are simplifying Security with zero attack service and zero trust planes and self sovereign data

. Enterprise Blockchain as a shared common single source of truth for Data within your Organisation and across Organisations

Yes this is more technology, once it is set up, and the Integration Patterns and Principles are in place it will be a case of "rinse and repeat".

What do you think ? How can we reduce the hidden costs of Simplification ?

Andy Silvey.

Author Bio:

Andy Silvey is a 25 years SAP Technology veteran [15 years SAP Basis and 10 years SAP Tech Arch including Tech, Integration, Security, Data from 3.1H to S/4HANA PCE on RISE and the BTP and everything in between, and former SCN Moderator and Mentor alumni].

Andy is also co-Founder of atkrypto  inc, an startup whose ambition is to make Blockchain easy for Enterprise.

atkrypto dot io's flagship product is the atkrypto Enterprise Blockchain Platform for SAP,  and atkrypto.io is a SAP Partner Edge Open EcoSystem Partner. 

The atkrypto Enterprise Blockchain Platform for SAP has been designed by SAP Independent Experts for the needs of SAP Customers and to be deployed on the SAP BTP Kyma Runtime Service and leverage native integration to SAP Products.

atkrypto Enterprise Blockchain Platform for SAP has a number of unique qualities, including being the only Blockchain software in the world which has a DataCenter version and a light mobile version which can run on Edge/IoT/Mobile devices and enables data to be written to the Blockchain at the Edge where that same Blockchain is running on a Server in the DataCenter, protecting the integrity and originality of data from the Edge to Insights. Taking Blockchain to the Data at the Edge instead of taking the Data to the Blockchain.

All of this makes atkrypto dot io the DePIN Decentralised Physical Infrastructure Network solution for Enterprise.

entry.title: Perform SAP Principal Propagation with Microsoft Entra ID for SAP SuccessFactors!
entry.content:
This blog is co-authored with Vinayak Adkoli (Lead Product Manager, SAP Integration Suite, SAP SE). Link to Microsoft Learn Hub for Power Platform and SAP here🔗.

Dear community,

The last blog post showed how to integrate Microsoft and SAP low code solutions with SAP services using the API Management capability of SAP Integration Suite and Microsoft Entra ID as identity provider overall. Todays post is about the specifics for SAP SuccessFactors.

Spoiler alert🤫: the approach stays the same! Only a slightly modified API Management policy needs to be imported. SuccessFactors uses API keys for its SAML assertions rather than an OAuth2 client id and contains your SuccessFactors company id.

 

Technically you could use the same policy for NetWeaver based systems and SuccessFactors, because the “unexpected” authentication attributes are ignored as of the releases that were tested. It is a good practice though to separate the concerns. Therefore we published a separate policy instead of a one-size-fits all one with bloated number of attributes.

At the core of the solution is again the proven OAuth2SAMLBearer flow. See the official SuccessFactors docs on the required SAML attributes for reference.

In addition to that, solving this challenge on Integration Suite level enables scaling the approach to arbitrary many different consumer solutions. No more re-inventing the wheel for every developer!

Find the SuccessFactors specific policy on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

Of course, you may deviate from the blueprint outlined based on your scenario across SAP BTP, SAP Graph, Integration Suite, other SAP SaaS solutions etc.

 

A glimpse under the hood

Start your journey on SAP API Management by creating a new API provider for your SuccessFactors tenant. Be aware☝️ of the different domains for the portal UI and the associated OData API endpoint! See SAP note 2215682 for reference.

 

Next, register your SuccessFactors OData API. In my sample I am taking the entity set “User”, that resides on the base path for a tenant in DC5 like so:

https://api5.successfactors.eu/odata/v2/User/$metadata

Make sure to verify the base path of the URL (/odata/v2 vs. /odata/v2/User) of your target endpoint on API Management. See below Screenshot for reference.

Otherwise, you will encounter routing issues for request like /odata/v2/User(‘1000’).

 

The API Management policy works under the assumption that trust between your SAP SuccessFactors OAuth2 server and Microsoft Entra ID has been setup before. See this guide for reference.

In addition to the existing authorizations maintained on SuccessFactors each application consuming the SAP API proxy from API Management need to be authorized on Entra ID.

 

See this official guide for details on the Entra ID setup. Be aware the linked guide refers to the SAML2 setup (not OAuth2!). See the difference for OAuth2SAMLBearer below:

 

The Entity ID can be any URI. It is a good practice though to use something that resembles your SuccessFactors tenant.

Be aware that Entity ID must be unique in your Entra ID tenant. In case you want to use SAML2 for SSO and OAuth2 for SAP Principal Propagation for this tenant at the same time, you need to maintain both on the Entra ID enterprise app registration. Assign an order (index) that works with your login flow.

 

Take care of your OAuth settings

The steps for the OAuth configuration may vary by SAP product. Here the focus is on SAP SuccessFactors.

Move on to your SuccessFactors tenant and open the app “Manage OAuth2 Client Applications” to create a new OAuth client for your Microsoft Entra ID enterprise app. Populate the values as per your environment.

Be aware that you need to copy the certificate body only without the header -----BEGIN CERTIFICATE----- and -----END CERTIFICATE-----.

 

Import the policy into your tenant

Download the policy from the SAP Business Accelerator Hub and import the template into your SAP API Management tenant.

 

Apply to all the PostFlow steps of the TargetEndpoints of your APIs as you see fit. See this SAP tutorial and this SAP GitHub repos for more details.

 

Consider the governance and security trade-offs between mighty policies and SAP OAuth clients authorized for everything vs. a multitude of clients for each API and scope.

 

Configure the policy using a key value map

All the configuration needed for earlier shown token exchange flow is best provided with an encoded key value map (I recommend starting with un-encoded one, when you do this the first time for simpler troubleshooting). Create a new encrypted one called “SAPPrincipalPropagationMap”. The name is referenced on the provided policy.

 

Fill the values as per your environment:

Key

Value sample

Hints

entra-id-tenant-id

12a345bc-1234-56ab-78ab-zzzzzzzzz

Find it on the Azure portal (e.g. here)

issuer

https://sts.windows.net/12a345bc-1234-56ab-78ab-zzzzzzzzz/

The prefix is fixed. Only the tenant id is dynamic.

 

https://login.microsoftonline.com/12a345bc-1234-56ab-78ab-zzzzzzzzz for v2 endpoints

entra-id-audience

api://999abce-7777-abcd-a6c9-zzzzzzzzzzz

The globally unique Application ID URI from the Entra ID app registration representing your SAP API Management instance

AADRegisteredAppClientId

999abce-7777-abcd-a6c9-zzzzzzzzzzz

The Application (client) id of the Entra ID app registration representing your SAP API Management instance.

AADRegisteredAppClientSecret

 

The secret created for the application 999abce-7777-abcd-a6c9-zzzzzzzzzzz

AADSAPResource

https://my.successfactors.eu

The provider’s name from your SuccessFactors SAML setup.

sap-oauth-sf-api-key

ABCDE1234FGH6789

API Key generated by SuccessFactors for your OAuth2 application. Instructions here.

sap-oauth-company-id

SF123456

Your SuccessFactors Company id. Find here.

SAPOAuthServerAdress

ForTokenEndpoint

salesdemo.successfactors.eu

OAuth endpoint of your successfactors instance.

 Adjust the name of the API provider as per your setup in the policy elements “fetchSAPOAuthToken”, and “GetCSRFToken”.

The final mile of integration

Ok, all homework is done. Now we get to go outside and enjoy the “low code” sun with SAP SuccessFactors 🌞Create your SAP OData connection in Power Automate, choose the authentication type Microsoft Entra ID and paste the URI of the Entra ID app registration that represents your API Management.

 

Hints on troubleshooting

  • Before applying the APIM policy consider running the sequence of authentication calls locally (with “line of sight” to NetWeaver of course) using a REST client. See this Postman collection for reference.

 

  • Verify the produced Entra ID tokens attributes using a safe JWT validator (e.g. DevToys). Don’t share your sensitive tokens on some website for validation!
    • iss (Issuer): Should read something like https://sts.windows.net/12a3456-zzz... or https://login.microsoftonline.com/12a3456-zzz... for v2 endpoints.
    • aud (Audience): Something like “api://bbbbbb-cccc-dddd-dddd-eeeeeeee”
    • scp (Scope): “user_impersonation”

 

  • For the SAML2 assertion exercise the same approach but do base64 decode and XML pretty print. Notepad++ with MIME tools -> Base64 decode and XML Tools -> pretty print does the job locally just fine. Again, don’t paste sensitive info online! Verify the following claims from your assertion:
    • AudienceRestriction -> Audience: Should be a URL containing your app id, e.g. https://my.successfactors.eu
    • Claims: Name, email or whatever you have configured to be used to identify the named SAP backend user.

 

  • While doing integration tests with the API Management policy consider decoding the key value map or use a public one till you are confident with your setup to see immediately what config values were provided.

 

  • Verify that your key value map changes are being pulled.

 

  • Cached a faulty token? Disable the caching policy step "LookupSAPTokens" using the enabled attribute on the XML or consider adding an API to clear the cache by user using the "InvalidateCache" policy step. 

 

  • 405 Method Not allowed (SAP note 3386802 SAP API Management generates PUT method entries for OData v2 and PATCH entries for OData v4 services. Power Automate's "Update entity" step uses the PATCH approach. In case of conflict, either choose the "Create any type of OData request" step and configure PUT instead on Power Automate or adjust the swagger definition using "Edit in API Designer" on SAP API Management to add PATCH to cater for this.

 

Thoughts on production readiness

SAP Integration Suite is more than ready for prime time as the de-facto standard for SAP heavy integrations.

The OAuthSAML2Bearer flow is an ever green discussed in the community at length for years and fully supported by SAP for service to service Principal Propagation.

The involved Entra ID app registration client secret can be governed with Azure automation or SAP Build Apps Process Automation. See this Microsoft article and this GitHub repos for reference.

Applying the battle-proven API management policy from the API hub ensures a configuration driven approach and clear update paths.

PowerAutomate also supports Principal Propagation with SuccessFactors directly. However, that would be a point-to-point integration without the governance and security benefits of having an API Management solution in between.

The SAP and Microsoft low code eco system is a natural fit for productivity across business needs that involve M365 (Microsoft Graph, Teams, Outlook, SharePoint Online, etc.) and SAP.

In terms of governance, SAP offers extensive integration with the Microsoft ecosystem. See this co-authored blog about SAP API Management integrating Azure APIs and this one about Azure API Center to handle a multitude of gateways in a single place.

 

Final Words

That’s a wrap 🌯. Today you saw how to extend the learnings from the first blog to enable SAP SuccessFactors for Power Automate with Principal Propagation through Microsoft Entra ID. The approach maps Microsoft identities to SAP named users to retain its SAP authorizations. In addition to that you learnt that a provided SAP API Management policy performs the heavy lifting of the authentication flow.

App developers and low coders no longer need to deal with the complexity of the principal propagation and get added benefit of token caching, and CSRF handling out-of-the-box.

Get started from here.

#Kudos to Martin Raepple for the contribution of the tricky part of the token dance.

Cheers

@vinayak_adkoli and Martin

entry.title: Output Management Solutions For LOBs In SAP Business Technology Platform To Keep Core Clean
entry.content:
In our LOBs systems like SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition,SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, SAP S/4HANA OnPremise, scope item Output Management (1LQ) will cover the requirements of rendering PDF form used by printing, email.
 
But there are scenarios where the requirements can not be realized with standard function of 1LQ,the following are some examples:
  • Missing data even tried with key user extensibility.
  • Missing functions like need digital signature in PDF form.
  • Customers need pull data from multiple systems to render PDF form in realtime.
  • Customers need a stand alone application for partners to print PDF form with data from LOB systems in realtime.
  • Customer need to render PDF form in mobile device with data from LOB systems in realtime.
  • Partners want to deploy SAAS solution for customers for Output Management.
  • Other scenarios.
Some scenarios can be realized with On-stack extensibility in ABAP environment. To keep core clean,cloud ABAP is recommended, classic technologies like smartform, SAPscript should be avoided. Adobe Forms technology is recommended.
 
To keep core clean, Side-By-Side extensibility in SAP Business Technology Platform(BTP) are highly recommended for loosely-coupled scenarios. For Output Management solutions in BTP, we have 2 styles solutions.
 
First style is automatically label printing or email. This means when business object like billing created in LOB system, the PDF form is automatically rendered with predefined format are automatically sent to a local printer or emailed to specific contact. I wrote a blog Build Digital Document Platform Backend Service On BTP with BTP Ads Sdm Print Service before. If we use the solution in this blog, we can realize automatically label printing with SAP Integration Suite or SAP Build Process Automation very efficiently. The following is a solution architecture for a real customer use case.

 
Second style is Side-By-Side extensibility with CAP or RAP as the backend, SAP UI5 as the front end.
Here will deploy a SAP UI5 application in SAP Build Work Zone or SAP BTP ABAP environment for the end users. The following is one of the architectures:

 

For this style, we prepared the following Discovery Center Missions for you:
  • If you prefer CAP in NodeJs, you can follow the mission Add Printing Labels to Your User Experience for SAP S/4HANA Cloud with CAP Node.js
  • If you prefer CAP in Java,you can follow the mission Add Printing Labels to Your User Experience for SAP S/4HANA Cloud with CAP Java
  •  If you prefer RAP(Cloud ABAP),you can follow the mission Add Printing Label to Your User Experience for SAP S/4HANA Cloud In BTP ABAP Environment
All the 3 missions are base on best practice of Side-By-Side extensibility. You can apply the same method in other Side-By-Side extensibility scenarios.
 
The mission Add Printing Label to Your User Experience for SAP S/4HANA Cloud is based on a little old technology. I will retire the mission.
 
To better help our customers to learn the solutions, we prepared the following webinars in December 2024. Welcome to register with the following linkages, we are happy to explain and answer queries and discuss.
 
Little remind: please reach out to your BTP Customer Success Manager to get the CRM ID for your organisation before the register.
  • 4TH December 2024, Add Printing Labels to Your User Experience for SAP S/4HANA Cloud with CAP Node.js
  • 11ST December 2024, Add Printing Labels to Your User Experience for SAP S/4HANA Cloud with CAP Java
These 2 solutions are based on CAP. If you are interested in CAP,I think you can start with the Learning Sources.



The Side-By-Side extensibility method used in the missions can be applied to other LOBs like SAP ECC,SAP SuccessFactors HCM,etc.

 

The ends!
Thanks for your time!

 

Best regards!
Jacky Liu

entry.title: The SAP S/4HANA PCE Private Cloud Edition on RISE & SAP BTP Toolbox for SAP Technology Architects 🧰
entry.content:
The SAP S/4HANA PCE Private Cloud Edition on RISE & SAP BTP: Toolbox for SAP Technology Architects 🧰

Back in 2013 when I was studying for SAP HANA Certification I published the following blog, as a central point of reference for SAP HANA technical information, it helped me with what I was studying and helped others with having piles of very useful information in one place:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/the-sap-hana-reference-for-sap-basis-administrators/ba-p/13007539

Fast forward 12 years, and we are now in the same situation with SAP S/4HANA PCE on RISE and the BTP, there is useful documentation all over the place and it would help if there was a central location for SAP Technology Architects to use as a "Toolbox". It will certainly help me 🙂

Implementing and Migrating to and Running S/4 PCE on RISE for the last two years, and as usual collecting a mountain of the most useful documentation as a personal toolbox, it's been clear to see that similar to the situation back in the earlier period of HANA, there is useful RISE documentation in a number of locations, this blog will act as a central resource pointing to all of the locations of the SAP S/4 PCE RISE documentation.

And similarly for the BTP, having been doing BTP (or as it was the SAP Cloud Platform) for 7 years, this blog will be a central location pointing to the most useful BTP documentation for SAP Technology Architects.

The two subjects are combined, SAP S/4HANA PCE on RISE with BTP, because in my eyes the two go hand in hand, S/4HANA PCE on RISE is the next generation of the previous On-Premise ERP offerings from SAP, and the BTP contains the next generation of Applications which we will all use with the S/4HANA PCE on RISE, including for example, and not limited to, Integration Suite and CPI & EM/AEM & APIM, obviously CALM as this is replacing in many but not all cases what we do with SolMan, and all of the other Services which we will need pretty much day by day to run S/4HANA PCE on RISE.

If you have links to useful documentation which you think will help others and should be added to the list, then please just write it in the comments and I will add it to the list.

Please expect to find changes and new information here, because it will be a live work in progress, it's going to take a few weeks to list and classify everything that I already have.

Tip: search the list for what you are looking for, eg FUE Licensing details

Here we go....

Section 6] SAP Customer Center of Excellence

(This section is a work in progress)

You might be asking yourself why in a SAP RISE Toolbox there are links to how to do a SAP CCOE ?

That's a great question, and the answer is that, most companies have been running/operating SAP the same way since 3.1h, and now with RISE the whole game is changing, the Roles and Responsibilities are changing and the Segregation of Duties is changing, between who does what, SAP, Customer, and if they have one the Customer's System Integrator. 

And that's why, during the S/4HANA RISE Transformation, you will be best off, if you take the opportunity and redesign how you run/operate SAP.

Andy Silvey.

SAP Technology Architect [Contractor]

Author Bio:

Andy Silvey is a 25 years SAP Technology veteran [15 years SAP Basis and 10 years SAP Tech Arch including Tech, Integration, Security, Data from 3.1H to S/4HANA PCE on RISE and the BTP and everything in between, and former SCN Moderator and Mentor alumni].

Andy is also co-Founder of atkrypto  inc, an startup which is making Blockchain easy for Enterprise.

atkrypto dot io's flagship product is the atkrypto Enterprise Blockchain Platform for SAP,  and atkrypto dot io is a SAP Partner Edge Open EcoSystem Partner. 

The atkrypto Enterprise Blockchain Platform for SAP has been designed by SAP Independent Experts for the needs of SAP Customers and to be deployed on the SAP BTP Kyma Runtime Service and leverage native integration to SAP Products.

atkrypto Enterprise Blockchain Platform for SAP has a number of unique qualities, including being the only Blockchain software in the world which has a DataCenter version and a light mobile version which can run on Edge/IoT/Mobile devices and enables data to be written to the Blockchain at the Edge where that same Blockchain is running on a Server in the DataCenter, protecting the integrity and originality of data from the Edge to Insights. Taking Blockchain to the Data at the Edge instead of taking the Data to the Blockchain.

All of this makes atkrypto dot io the DePIN Decentralised Physical Infrastructure Network solution for Enterprise.

entry.title: My Home in SAP S/4HANA - A Deep Dive as of 2408
entry.content:
My Home is your personalized entry page within SAP S/4HANA,  that provides you with easy access to the business insights, tasks, and applications you need to do your job with greater speed and agility. My Home lets you access and manage your to-dos, your favorite pages, your favorite apps, and your insights on any device. It builds on the functionality of the SAP Fiori launchpad and leverages Spaces and Pages (as a prerequisite). The new My Home was first released with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2208 release. Since its first release, there have been great improvements in its functionality and user experience. In this blog, we’ll revisit the features of My Home that will enhance your user experience and productivity.  

Video: Welcome to the My Home in SAP S/4HANA 

My Home in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

 

 

My Home Sections 

 

The My Home entry page is divided into different sections. You can personalize the content of each section to fit your specific needs. 

 

To-Dos 

The To-Dos section provides an overview of your daily tasks. It combines your tasks from the My Inbox application and your situations from the standard and extended My Situations applications. The tasks display relevant business information about your assigned workflow items. It enables you to process your tasks without ever having to leave My Home. If Collaborative ERP is enabled on the system, you can collaborate directly with colleagues via MS Teams to resolve issues about your tasks quickly.  

Video: Managing the To-Dos Section 

SAP S/4HANA My Home To Do Section

 

 

 

News 

The News section keeps you updated on your company's latest news, or SAP S/4HANA system news. If SAP S/4HANA system news is displaying, you can shuffle through system news from different lines of business. You can manage SAP S/4HANA system news by choosing a line of business  that interests you to stay focused on relevant information. 

Video: Personalizing the News section 

SAP S/4HANA My Home News Section

 

 

 SAP S/4HANA My Home System News articles

 

 

 

Pages  

The Pages section provides you with quick access to your favorite or most used pages. You can manage the visibility of your pages and order them in a way that enhances your productivity.  

Video: Personalizing the Pages section 

SAP S/4HANA My Home Pages Section

 

 

 

Apps 

The Apps section provides easy access to your favorite, most used, recently used, and recommended applications. The Apps section improves the discoverability of your applications. The Recommended tab offers app suggestions using SAP Business AI, based on your user-specific role assignment. You can add apps to the Favorites tab from your most used, recently used, and recommended applications. You can organize and categorize your favorite applications by using the grouping, ordering and color-coding functionality so you can access them even more efficiently. 

Video: Personalizing the Apps section 

 SAP S/4HANA My Home Apps Section

 

 

 

Insights Tiles and Insights Cards 

The Insights section allows you to manage and monitor your company’s activities. It shows critical business information through KPIs and micro charts. This section is broken into two sections: Insights Tiles and Insights Cards. Insights cards and tiles display dynamic content at a glance. They provide quick and easy access to the applications that can provide more detailed information. Insights tiles offer analytical information, such as charts or KPIs. Insights cards are similar to Insights tiles but provide more detailed analytical information in the form of charts, tables or lists from supporting overview page-, list report-, and analytical list page-based applications. Thanks to this information, you can assess what is happening at any time and take action when needed.  You can customize the Insights section to display information that enables you to do your job with speed and agility. 

Video: Personalizing the Insights Tiles Section 

Video: Personalizing the Insights Cards Section 

 SAP S/4HANA My Home Insights Section

 

 

The My Home Layout and Advanced Settings 

 You can personalize the individual My Home sections, and another key feature is the ability to customize the layout of these sections on My Home. This personalization capability allows you to prioritize the information that is most important to you. You can manage the visibility and the display order of each of the My Home sections. 

Video: Personalizing the Layout of My Home  

Once you have personalized the My Home sections you can easily share your personalized content with co-workers who have the same or a similar role as you. This  enables them to also work with speed and agility. 

Video: Sharing the Personalized Content 

 

 

 

Now that we have given you a recap of the available My Home functionality in SAP S/4HANA, we hope you enjoy working with My Home in SAP S/4HANA and reap its benefits. You can  look forward to more enhancements that will improve your My Home experience even further in future releases of SAP S/4HANA.  

Note: Innovations appear first in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. The demos in the videos were recorded on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition in September 2024. 

Stay tuned! 

Caroline Welsh and Anna Freitag 

Product Managers of My Home, Cloud ERP UX Engineering.
